title: Account Authorization
description: The authenticated caller's grant against a specific account: which tasks are callable, which request fields are modifiable, and the named scope (if any) the grant maps to. Attached to per-account entries in sync_accounts and list_accounts responses when the seller supports scope introspection. Absence means the seller does not advertise introspectable scope — it does NOT mean 'no restrictions' and callers MUST NOT infer access from absence. Scope can change between calls; the authorization object describes the caller's grant at the moment the response was generated. Conceptually analogous to RFC 7662 OAuth 2.0 Token Introspection, specialized for AdCP's task-and-field authorization model.

allowed_tasks.description: Canonical snake_case task names the caller may invoke against this account (e.g., get_media_buys, update_media_buy, create_media_buy, sync_creatives). Absence of a task from this list MUST be interpreted as 'not permitted' — invoking an absent task MUST return SCOPE_INSUFFICIENT. This list reflects the caller's grant, not the seller's universal capability surface (for that, see get_adcp_capabilities). A seller may grant narrower subsets to different callers on the same account.

field_scopes.description: Optional per-task allowlist of request fields the caller may set. Keys are task names (which MUST also appear in allowed_tasks). Values are arrays of top-level request-field paths permitted for that task. When a task appears in field_scopes, requests to that task with any field outside the allowlist MUST be rejected with FIELD_NOT_PERMITTED. Implicit framing fields are always permitted and do NOT need to appear in the allowlist: `account`, `media_buy_id`, `package_id`, `creative_id`, `revision`, `idempotency_key`, `buyer_ref`, `po_number`, `dry_run`, `context`, `ext`, `adcp_major_version`, and `push_notification_config` (transport-level async receipt, not business state). Tasks absent from field_scopes have no field-level restriction beyond what the task schema already enforces. An entry with an empty array means 'framing fields only, no business fields' — semantically distinct from the task being absent from field_scopes.

scope_name.description: Optional named scope identifier. When present, callers and sellers can reason about the grant by name rather than by enumerating allowed_tasks and field_scopes. Modeled as a discriminated union so code generators produce a literal type for the standard scope(s) and a distinct type for seller-specific values — this prevents a typo of `attestation_verifier` from being silently accepted as a custom scope. Seller-specific scope names MUST use a `seller:` prefix to avoid collision with future standard scopes.

anyOf.title: StandardScope
anyOf.description: Standard named scopes defined by the AdCP spec. `attestation_verifier` binds to Tier-2 Production Verified conformance.

anyOf.title: SellerScope
anyOf.description: Seller-defined scope name, prefixed with `seller:`. Buyers MUST NOT assume any semantics from a seller-prefixed scope name.

read_only.description: Convenience flag. When true, the caller is permitted only non-mutating tasks. Sellers MUST reject any mutation from a read-only caller with READ_ONLY_SCOPE. Sellers MAY omit this field; omission is equivalent to `false`. Callers MUST NOT infer read-only from `allowed_tasks` alone — the seller MUST set this explicitly when it applies.
